content.content:
1680	3. Fetch dataset items via `client.dataset(dataset_id).listItems({ clean: true })`.
1681	4. Parse per-scrape_mode (see §3e table) — set `follower_count`/`avg_views_last_10`/etc. columns the mode owns, leave others null.
1682	5. **Guarded terminal-state UPDATE** — crucial for Apify's retry semantics, which can deliver `SUCCEEDED` first and a retried `ABORTED` for the same `run_id` later (or vice versa). The update only transitions OUT OF `refreshing`, never between terminal states:
1683	   ```sql
1684	   update public.metric_snapshots
1685	      set status        = :status_next,         -- 'fresh' | 'failed'
1686	          raw_payload   = :items,
1687	          error_message = :error_message,
1688	          -- fetched_at stamped at run-completion, not at webhook-arrival,
1689	          -- so retries don't shift the timestamp forward.
1690	          fetched_at    = :apify_finished_at
1691	    where apify_run_id = :run_id
1692	      and status       = 'refreshing';          -- guard: do nothing if already terminal
1693	   ```
1694	   If zero rows are affected, log a `webhook_duplicate` event and return `200 OK` — idempotent no-op. The first terminal signal wins; later retries are ignored. This keeps a fresh snapshot from being clobbered by a retried-ABORTED.
1695	6. Trigger fires `denorm_metrics()` → updates only the `creator_profiles` columns owned by this `scrape_mode`. (Note: `denorm_metrics()` already short-circuits when the new row's status is not `fresh` — see §15 trigger — so the no-op guard in step 5 also keeps the denorm path clean.)
1696	7. On failure: increment `social_links.fail_count`; if ≥3 consecutive failures across all scrape_modes → `social_links.status=failed_fetch` + notify creator. On success: reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects *uninterrupted* failures.
1697	
1698	**IG consistency note:** Because IG needs two runs per refresh, `creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` will bump *twice* per refresh (once per webhook). The UI treats "fresh" as `min(metrics_fetched_at_across_modes)`. If one run fails and the other succeeds, the creator still sees a partial update — the failed mode's old value remains stale-but-shown, with a subtle warning chip. (Detail reconciled via `metric_snapshots.status` per mode.)
1699	
1700	### 5.4 Listings — Browse (Creator)
1701	
1702	**`GET /rest/v1/listings?status=eq.active&...`** — PostgREST direct. Client sends its cached creator metrics as filter params (generated columns make this cheap).
1703	
1704	**`GET /listings/:id`** — Edge Function
1705	```ts
1706	interface Res {
1707	  listing: Listing;
1708	  conditions: ListingCondition[];
1709	  sample_videos: SampleVideo[];
1710	  eligibility: {
1711	    eligible: boolean;
1712	    failed_conditions: Array<{ metric; required; actual }>;
1713	    has_active_application: boolean;
1714	  };
1715	}
1716	```
1717	
1718	### 5.5 Listings — Manage (Lister)
1719	
1720	**`POST /listings`**
1721	```ts
1722	interface Req {
1723	  title: string; description?: string;
1724	  price_cents: number; currency?: string;
1725	  max_submissions?: number | null;
1726	  end_date?: string | null;
1727	  pre_conditions: ListingConditionInput[];
1728	  post_conditions: ListingConditionInput[];
1729	  sample_videos: { platform: Platform; url: string; caption?: string; }[];
1730	  publish: boolean;
1731	}
1732	interface Res { listing_id: string; version_id: string; }
1733	```
1734	
1735	**`PATCH /listings/:id`**
1736	```ts
1737	interface Req { /* subset of create */ }
1738	interface Res { listing_id: string; version_id: string; cascaded_applications: number; }
1739	```
1740	
1741	**`POST /listings/:id/status`**
1742	```ts
1743	interface Req { action: 'publish' | 'pause' | 'resume' | 'close' | 'archive'; }
1744	interface Res { ok: true; new_status: ListingStatus; }
1745	```
1746	
1747	### 5.6 Applications
1748	
1749	**`POST /applications`**
1750	```ts
1751	interface Req { listing_id: string; cover_note?: string; }
1752	interface Res { application_id: string; listing_version_id: string; }
1753	```
1754	Server re-evaluates eligibility; notifies lister.
1755	Errors: `403 INELIGIBLE`, `409 ALREADY_APPLIED`, `409 LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE`, `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED`.
1756	
1757	**Concurrency contract — closes the race with `bump_listing_version`.** Without row locks, an applicant's `INSERT applications` can interleave with a lister's `UPDATE listings` that triggers the version bump + cascade-cancel, leaving a fresh `pending` row pinned to an already-stale version. The insert txn must serialize on the listing row:
1758	
1759	```sql
1760	begin;
1761	-- 1. Pin the listing to this txn. Both POST /applications and the edge-function
1762	--    wrapper of bump_listing_version must take this lock. The bump trigger runs
1763	--    under the UPDATE's own row lock, so they are mutually exclusive.
1764	select id, status, current_version_id
1765	  from public.listings
1766	 where id = :listing_id
1767	 for update;
1768	
1769	-- 2. Abort if the listing moved out of 'active' in that window.
1770	-- 3. Re-run eligibility against the NOW-current version_id (not the one the
1771	--    client thought was current when it opened the confirm sheet).
1772	-- 4. Insert applications.listing_version_id = the locked current_version_id.
1773	commit;
1774	```
1775	
1776	If the lister's version bump commits first, the creator's txn reads the new `current_version_id` after acquiring the lock; the eligibility rules may now differ, so the edge function re-checks and returns `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` with the new version's diff, which the client surfaces as "Terms just changed — review before applying" on Campaign Detail. No silent commit against a stale version is possible.
1777	
1778	**`POST /applications/:id/withdraw`** — creator
1779	**`POST /applications/:id/approve`** — lister
1780	```ts
1781	interface Req { override_ineligible?: boolean; decision_note?: string; }
1782	```
1783	May return `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`.
1784	**`POST /applications/:id/reject`** — lister
1785	```ts
1786	interface Req { decision_note?: string; }
1787	```
1788	
1789	### 5.7 Submissions
1790	
1791	**`POST /applications/:id/submissions`**
1792	```ts
1793	interface Req { videos: { platform: Platform; url: string }[]; cover_note?: string; }
1794	interface Res { submission_id: string; }
1795	```
1796	Validates URLs via oEmbed; creates submission + videos; notifies lister.
1797	Errors: `403 APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED`, `409 SUBMISSION_EXISTS`, `422 INVALID_VIDEO_URL`.
1798	
1799	**`POST /submissions/:id/approve`** — lister
1800	**`POST /submissions/:id/reject`** — lister
1801	```ts
1802	interface Req { decision_note?: string; }
1803	```
1804	
1805	### 5.8 Notifications
1806	
1807	**`GET /rest/v1/notifications`** — PostgREST direct.
1808	**`POST /notifications/mark-read`**
1809	```ts
1810	interface Req { ids: string[] | 'all'; }
1811	interface Res { updated: number; }
1812	```
1813	**Realtime** — `supabase.channel('notif').on('postgres_changes', {filter: 'user_id=eq.<me>'}, ...)`.
1814	
1815	### 5.9 Realtime Channels
1816	
1817	| Channel | Who | Events |
1818	|---|---|---|
1819	| `notifications:user_id=<me>` | both | INSERT |
1820	| `applications:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
1821	| `submissions:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
1822	| `applications:creator_id=<me>` | creator | UPDATE |
1823	| `submissions:creator_id=<me>` | creator | UPDATE |
1824	| `metric_snapshots:social_link_id=<id>` | creator | INSERT, UPDATE (push metrics-ready) |
1825	
1826	**RLS enforcement on Realtime:** Supabase Realtime runs every broadcast row through the table's SELECT policy before sending, so a creator who subscribes to `applications:creator_id=<otherId>` receives zero rows — the underlying policy `creator_id = auth.jwt()->>'sub'::uuid` filters rows at the database, not client-side. Channel filters in the client are an optimization, not a security boundary. Manual test plan for launch: subscribe from Creator A's JWT to a channel filtered on Creator B's id, perform a write against Creator B's data, assert zero events delivered. Automate this in the integration suite.
1827	
1828	---
1829

1830	## 6. Eligibility Evaluation Engine
1831	
1832	### 6.1 Condition Catalog
1833	
1834	| metric | platform | operator | threshold |
1835	|---|---|---|---|
1836	| `min_followers` | tt \| ig | gte | numeric |
1837	| `min_avg_views_last_n` | tt \| ig | gte | numeric |
1838	| `min_total_likes` | tt | gte | numeric |
1839	| `min_videos_posted` | tt \| ig | gte | numeric |
1840	| `verified_only` | tt | bool | boolean (IG can't detect reliably from scrape) |
1841	
1842	### 6.2 Evaluator Signature
1843	
1844	```ts
1845	type Metric = 'min_followers' | 'min_avg_views_last_n' | 'min_total_likes' | 'min_videos_posted' | 'verified_only';
1846	interface Condition { metric: Metric; platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; operator: 'gte' | 'bool'; threshold: number | boolean; }
1847	interface CreatorMetrics {
1848	  tiktok: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; total_likes?: number; video_count?: number; is_verified?: boolean; fetched_at?: string } | null;
1849	  instagram: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; media_count?: number; fetched_at?: string } | null;
1850	}
1851	interface EligibilityResult {
1852	  eligible: boolean;
1853	  failed: Array<{condition: Condition; actual: number | boolean | null}>;
1854	  stale_platforms: string[];
1855	}
1856	function evaluate(conditions: Condition[], metrics: CreatorMetrics): EligibilityResult { ... }
1857	```
1858	
1859	### 6.3 Where It Runs
1860	
1861	- **Client:** on app resume, loads `creator_profiles` into context. Feed inlines `pre_conditions` per listing; filters locally. Instant "eligible only" toggle.
1862	- **Server (authoritative):** `POST /applications` runs the same TS function on Deno.
1863	- **Approve-time re-check:** `POST /applications/:id/approve` re-runs. Returns `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with override.
1864	
1865	### 6.4 Scaling Feed to 1000+ Listings
1866	
1867	- `listings.min_followers_tiktok/ig` generated (MAX of pre-conditions per platform), btree-indexed.
1868	- Feed query:
1869	  ```sql
1870	  select * from public.listings
1871	  where status = 'active'
1872	    and (min_followers_tiktok is null or min_followers_tiktok <= $creator_tt_followers)
1873	    and (min_followers_instagram is null or min_followers_instagram <= $creator_ig_followers)
1874	  order by created_at desc
1875	  limit 50;
1876	  ```
1877	- Complex conditions (avg views, video count) evaluated client-side after first-pass server filter.
1878	
1879	---
1880	
1881	## 7. Critical Workflow Sequence Diagrams
1882	
1883	### 7.1 Creator Signup + Initial Apify Scrape
1884	
1885	```
1886	App           EdgeFn(signup-creator)       Apify API          Our Webhook Fn        DB
1887	 │─POST /auth/signup-creator {username, tt, ig}──>│                                    │
1888	 │                                                  │─INSERT users, creator_profile─────>│
1889	 │                                                  │─INSERT social_links (tt, ig)───────>│

file.content:
## 6. Eligibility Evaluation Engine

### 6.1 Condition Catalog

| metric | platform | operator | threshold |
|---|---|---|---|
| `min_followers` | tt \| ig | gte | numeric |
| `min_avg_views_last_n` | tt \| ig | gte | numeric |
| `min_total_likes` | tt | gte | numeric |
| `min_videos_posted` | tt \| ig | gte | numeric |
| `verified_only` | tt | bool | boolean (IG can't detect reliably from scrape) |

### 6.2 Evaluator Signature

```ts
type Metric = 'min_followers' | 'min_avg_views_last_n' | 'min_total_likes' | 'min_videos_posted' | 'verified_only';
interface Condition { metric: Metric; platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; operator: 'gte' | 'bool'; threshold: number | boolean; }
interface CreatorMetrics {
  tiktok: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; total_likes?: number; video_count?: number; is_verified?: boolean; fetched_at?: string } | null;
  instagram: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; media_count?: number; fetched_at?: string } | null;
}
interface EligibilityResult {
  eligible: boolean;
  failed: Array<{condition: Condition; actual: number | boolean | null}>;
  stale_platforms: string[];
}
function evaluate(conditions: Condition[], metrics: CreatorMetrics): EligibilityResult { ... }
```

### 6.3 Where It Runs

- **Client:** on app resume, loads `creator_profiles` into context. Feed inlines `pre_conditions` per listing; filters locally. Instant "eligible only" toggle.
- **Server (authoritative):** `POST /applications` runs the same TS function on Deno.
- **Approve-time re-check:** `POST /applications/:id/approve` re-runs. Returns `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with override.

### 6.4 Scaling Feed to 1000+ Listings

- `listings.min_followers_tiktok/ig` generated (MAX of pre-conditions per platform), btree-indexed.
- Feed query:
  ```sql
  select * from public.listings
  where status = 'active'
    and (min_followers_tiktok is null or min_followers_tiktok <= $creator_tt_followers)
    and (min_followers_instagram is null or min_followers_instagram <= $creator_ig_followers)
  order by created_at desc
  limit 50;
  ```
- Complex conditions (avg views, video count) evaluated client-side after first-pass server filter.

---

## 7. Critical Workflow Sequence Diagrams

### 7.1 Creator Signup + Initial Apify Scrape

```
App           EdgeFn(signup-creator)       Apify API          Our Webhook Fn        DB
 │─POST /auth/signup-creator {username, tt, ig}──>│                                    │
 │                                                  │─INSERT users, creator_profile─────>│
 │                                                  │─INSERT social_links (tt, ig)───────>│

input.prompt:
I need to implement a new Supabase edge function `supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts` for the Marketify project. Before I write code, please summarize the exact patterns I must follow by reading these existing references.

**Goal of US-039:** Edge function `get-listing-detail` that returns for a given `listing_id`:
- `listing` row
- `conditions` (pre and post) from `listing_conditions` table
- `sample_videos`
- `eligibility: { eligible: boolean, failed_conditions: [{metric, required, actual}], has_active_application: boolean }`

Requires a creator JWT. Eligibility rules are per `docs/tech-architecture.md` §6 (Eligibility Evaluation Engine, lines ~1830-1880) and the response shape is documented at §5.4 (around line 1704).

**Please investigate and report (under 500 words):**

1. **Existing edge function patterns** — read `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`, `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`, and `supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts` (the latter is a good reference; creator-only JWT gated, returns JSON). Report: exact import paths, JWT verification call shape, CORS handling, OPTIONS pre-flight, error response shape (`{error: CODE}` + status).

2. **Listing schema** — run the Supabase MCP `list_tables` or look at the latest applied migration for `listings`, `listing_versions`, `listing_conditions`, `listing_sample_videos` (or equivalent). Report: column names on `listings` (especially `id`, `current_version_id`, `status`, `lister_id`, `title`, `description`, `price_cents`, `currency`, `max_submissions`, `end_date`, `created_at`), column names on `listing_conditions` (especially `listing_id` or `version_id`, `condition_kind` 'pre'|'post', `metric`, `platform`, `operator`, `threshold_int`, `threshold_bool`), and whatever `sample_videos` table exists (shape). Also check if `listing_versions` is where sample_videos live or if there's a separate table.

3. **Creator metrics** — the denormalized columns live on `creator_profiles`. Report the exact column names for each platform's metrics (especially tiktok_follower_count, tiktok_total_likes, tiktok_video_count, tiktok_is_verified, tiktok_avg_views_last_10, instagram_follower_count, instagram_avg_views_last_10, instagram_media_count, plus the per-platform fetched_at).

4. **Applications table** — what columns identify an "active" application for a given creator+listing? Report the relevant columns and status enum values ('pending', 'approved', etc.) and which statuses count as "active" for the `has_active_application` flag.

5. **Smoke test patterns** — look at `scripts/test/metrics-refresh.ts` and `scripts/test/auth-signup-creator.ts` to understand: how JWTs are signed with MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET, how seed fixtures are referenced, and how tests are structured.

6. **Seed data** — is there a seed script (`scripts/sql/seed.sql` or similar) that I can rely on for existing fixtures (e.g. a creator with known metrics, an active listing with known thresholds)? If so, list the key seed IDs I can use.

Do not write or modify any files. Return a focused summary with the specific facts I'll need to write the edge function and its smoke test. Include file paths + line numbers where helpful.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive summary.

---

## Summary: Marketify Edge Function Patterns & Schema (US-039)

Below are the exact patterns and schema details you need to implement `get-listing-detail`.

### 1. Edge Function Patterns

**Import paths & JWT verification** (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` lines 115–160):
- Import: `import { verifyJwt, type JwtClaims } from "../_shared/jwt.ts";`
- Call: `const claims = await verifyJwt(token);` (throws on invalid/expired)
- Claims shape: `{ sub: string; role: "creator" | "lister"; session_id: string; iat: number; exp: number }`
- Extract JWT from header: parse `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (see `metrics-refresh/index.ts` lines 129–134)

**CORS handling** (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` lines 11–16):
- Import: `import { corsHeaders } from "../_shared/cors.ts";`
- Apply to every response: `headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" }`
- OPTIONS pre-flight: `if (req.method === "OPTIONS") return new Response(null, { status: 204, headers: corsHeaders });` (line 137–138 of metrics-refresh)

**Error response shape** (per metrics-refresh lines 122–141):
- JSON: `{ error: "CODE" }` with appropriate status
- Common codes: `UNAUTHORIZED` (401), `FORBIDDEN` (403), `NOT_FOUND` (404), `INVALID_REQUEST` (400), `INVALID_JSON` (400)
- Patterns: `jsonResponse(status, { error: "CODE" })` where `jsonResponse(status, body)` returns a `Response` with corsHeaders + json

**Authenticating creator-only** (metrics-refresh lines 147–155):
```ts
let claims;
try {
  claims = await verifyJwt(token);
} catch {
  return jsonResponse(401, { error: "UNAUTHORIZED" });
}
if (claims.role !== "creator") {
  return jsonResponse(403, { error: "FORBIDDEN" });
}
```

---

### 2. Listing Schema

**`listings` table** (20260417100000_us_005, lines 10–32):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `lister_id` (uuid FK → users), `status` (enum: draft|active|paused|closed|archived)
- `title` (text), `description` (text), `category` (text), `price_cents` (int), `currency` (text)
- `max_submissions` (int, nullable), `approved_submissions_count`, `active_pending_applications_count`
- `current_version_id` (uuid FK → listing_versions), `version_number` (int)
- `min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_instagram` (int, nullable; denormalized MAX of pre-conditions per platform)
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `published_at`, `closed_at`

**`listing_versions` table** (lines 47–59):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `listing_id` (FK), `version_number` (int, unique per listing)
- `price_cents`, `currency`, `max_submissions`, `snapshot` (jsonb)
- Conditions and sample_videos reference `listing_versions.id`

**`listing_conditions` table** (lines 71–86):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `listing_version_id` (uuid FK → listing_versions)
- `kind` (enum: 'pre' | 'post'), `metric` (enum; see below), `platform` (enum: 'tiktok' | 'instagram', nullable)
- `operator` (text: 'gte'|'lte'|'eq'|'contains'|'bool')
- `numeric_threshold`, `text_threshold`, `bool_threshold` (store per metric type)

**`sample_videos` table** (lines 90–99):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `listing_version_id` (uuid FK), `platform` (enum: 'tiktok'|'instagram')
- `url` (text), `caption` (text), `sort_order` (smallint)

**`condition_metric` enum** (20260417080000_us_003, lines 37–49):
```
'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes', 'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec', 'post_min_video_count',
'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly', 'post_must_tag_account'
```

---

### 3. Creator Metrics (in `creator_profiles`)

**Column names** (20260417090000_us_004, lines 26–42):
- TikTok: `tiktok_follower_count`, `tiktok_avg_views_last_10`, `tiktok_total_likes`, `tiktok_video_count`, `tiktok_is_verified`
- Instagram: `instagram_follower_count`, `instagram_avg_views_last_10`, `instagram_media_count`
- Timestamp: `metrics_fetched_at` (shared across all platforms)

**Denormalization source** (20260417120000_us_007, lines 108–133):
- `metric_snapshots` (id, social_link_id, scrape_mode, status, follower_count, avg_views_last_10, total_likes, video_count, is_verified, fetched_at)
- Three scrape modes: `tiktok_profile`, `ig_details`, `ig_posts`
- Denormalization triggers on INSERT when `status='fresh'` and `is_latest=true`

---

### 4. Applications Table

**`applications` table** (20260417110000_us_006, lines 14–30):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `listing_id` (FK), `listing_version_id` (FK), `creator_id` (FK → users)
- `status` (enum: 'pending'|'approved'|'rejected'|'withdrawn'|'cancelled_listing_edit'|'cancelled_listing_closed')
- `cover_note`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `decided_at`, `decision_note`
- **Partial unique index:** `(listing_id, creator_id) where status in ('pending','approved')` — only one open application per creator+listing
- **For `has_active_application`:** Query where `status IN ('pending', 'approved')` and `creator_id = $creator_id` and `listing_id = $listing_id`

---

### 5. Eligibility Evaluation Rules

**From tech-architecture.md §6.1–6.2 (lines 1830–1856):**

| Metric | Platform | Operator | Threshold Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `min_followers` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `min_avg_views_last_n` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `min_total_likes` | tiktok | gte | numeric |
| `min_videos_posted` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |

const encoder = new TextEncoder();

function b64url(bytes: Uint8Array): string {

  for (const b of bytes) s += String.fromCharCode(b);
  return btoa(s).replaceAll("+", "-").replaceAll("/", "_").replace(/=+$/, "");
}

async function signMarketifyJwt(
  claims: { sub: string; role: "creator" | "lister"; session_id: string },
  secret: string,
): Promise<string> {

    { name: "HMAC", hash: "SHA-256" },
    false,
    ["sign"],

}
```

- `sample_videos`
- `eligibility: { eligible: boolean, failed_conditions: [{metric, required, actual}], has_active_application: boolean }`

Report: exact import paths, JWT verification call shape, CORS handling, OPTIONS pre-flight, error response shape (`{error: CODE}` + status).

Also check if `listing_versions` is where sample_videos live or if there's a separate table.

Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive summary.

---

## Summary: Marketify Edge Function Patterns & Schema (US-039)

Below are the exact patterns and schema details you need to implement `get-listing-detail`.

### 1. Edge Function Patterns

**Import paths & JWT verification** (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` lines 115–160):
- Import: `import { verifyJwt, type JwtClaims } from "../_shared/jwt.ts";`
- Call: `const claims = await verifyJwt(token);` (throws on invalid/expired)
- Claims shape: `{ sub: string; role: "creator" | "lister"; session_id: string; iat: number; exp: number }`
- Extract JWT from header: parse `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (see `metrics-refresh/index.ts` lines 129–134)

**CORS handling** (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` lines 11–16):
- Import: `import { corsHeaders } from "../_shared/cors.ts";`
- Apply to every response: `headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" }`
- OPTIONS pre-flight: `if (req.method === "OPTIONS") return new Response(null, { status: 204, headers: corsHeaders });` (line 137–138 of metrics-refresh)

**Error response shape** (per metrics-refresh lines 122–141):
- JSON: `{ error: "CODE" }` with appropriate status
- Common codes: `UNAUTHORIZED` (401), `FORBIDDEN` (403), `NOT_FOUND` (404), `INVALID_REQUEST` (400), `INVALID_JSON` (400)
- Patterns: `jsonResponse(status, { error: "CODE" })` where `jsonResponse(status, body)` returns a `Response` with corsHeaders + json

**Authenticating creator-only** (metrics-refresh lines 147–155):
```ts
let claims;
try {
  claims = await verifyJwt(token);
} catch {
  return jsonResponse(401, { error: "UNAUTHORIZED" });
}
if (claims.role !== "creator") {
  return jsonResponse(403, { error: "FORBIDDEN" });
}
```

---

### 2. Listing Schema

**`listings` table** (20260417100000_us_005, lines 10–32):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `lister_id` (uuid FK → users), `status` (enum: draft|active|paused|closed|archived)
- `title` (text), `description` (text), `category` (text), `price_cents` (int), `currency` (text)
- `max_submissions` (int, nullable), `approved_submissions_count`, `active_pending_applications_count`
- `current_version_id` (uuid FK → listing_versions), `version_number` (int)
- `min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_instagram` (int, nullable; denormalized MAX of pre-conditions per platform)
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `published_at`, `closed_at`

**`listing_versions` table** (lines 47–59):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `listing_id` (FK), `version_number` (int, unique per listing)
- `price_cents`, `currency`, `max_submissions`, `snapshot` (jsonb)
- Conditions and sample_videos reference `listing_versions.id`

**`listing_conditions` table** (lines 71–86):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `listing_version_id` (uuid FK → listing_versions)
- `kind` (enum: 'pre' | 'post'), `metric` (enum; see below), `platform` (enum: 'tiktok' | 'instagram', nullable)
- `operator` (text: 'gte'|'lte'|'eq'|'contains'|'bool')
- `numeric_threshold`, `text_threshold`, `bool_threshold` (store per metric type)

**`sample_videos` table** (lines 90–99):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `listing_version_id` (uuid FK), `platform` (enum: 'tiktok'|'instagram')
- `url` (text), `caption` (text), `sort_order` (smallint)

**`condition_metric` enum** (20260417080000_us_003, lines 37–49):
```
'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes', 'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec', 'post_min_video_count',
'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly', 'post_must_tag_account'
```

---

### 3. Creator Metrics (in `creator_profiles`)

**Column names** (20260417090000_us_004, lines 26–42):
- TikTok: `tiktok_follower_count`, `tiktok_avg_views_last_10`, `tiktok_total_likes`, `tiktok_video_count`, `tiktok_is_verified`
- Instagram: `instagram_follower_count`, `instagram_avg_views_last_10`, `instagram_media_count`
- Timestamp: `metrics_fetched_at` (shared across all platforms)

**Denormalization source** (20260417120000_us_007, lines 108–133):
- `metric_snapshots` (id, social_link_id, scrape_mode, status, follower_count, avg_views_last_10, total_likes, video_count, is_verified, fetched_at)
- Three scrape modes: `tiktok_profile`, `ig_details`, `ig_posts`
- Denormalization triggers on INSERT when `status='fresh'` and `is_latest=true`

---

### 4. Applications Table

**`applications` table** (20260417110000_us_006, lines 14–30):
- `id` (uuid, PK), `listing_id` (FK), `listing_version_id` (FK), `creator_id` (FK → users)
- `status` (enum: 'pending'|'approved'|'rejected'|'withdrawn'|'cancelled_listing_edit'|'cancelled_listing_closed')
- `cover_note`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `decided_at`, `decision_note`
- **Partial unique index:** `(listing_id, creator_id) where status in ('pending','approved')` — only one open application per creator+listing
- **For `has_active_application`:** Query where `status IN ('pending', 'approved')` and `creator_id = $creator_id` and `listing_id = $listing_id`

---

### 5. Eligibility Evaluation Rules

**From tech-architecture.md §6.1–6.2 (lines 1830–1856):**

| Metric | Platform | Operator | Threshold Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `min_followers` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `min_avg_views_last_n` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `min_total_likes` | tiktok | gte | numeric |
| `min_videos_posted` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `verified_only` | tiktok | bool | boolean |

**Response shape** (§5.4, line 1710–1714):
```ts
eligibility: {
  eligible: boolean;
  failed_conditions: Array<{ metric: string; required: number | boolean; actual: number | boolean | null }>;
  has_active_application: boolean;
}
```

**Evaluation** (§6.3):
- Run on creator_profiles denormalized metrics (tiktok_* + instagram_*)
- Pre-conditions: evaluated on all posts; post-conditions: evaluated on submissions
- For this endpoint: return pre-conditions in `failed_conditions`

content.content:
1	// Shared CORS headers for every Marketify edge function.
2	//
3	// Contract: exports `corsHeaders` (Record<string, string>) to splat into
4	//           every edge function's response and a pre-flight short-circuit.
5	// Auth:     n/a — the browser reads these before the request runs.
6	//
7	// `*` origin is safe here: edge functions authenticate via the
8	// Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt> header, not cookies, so there is no
9	// ambient-credential risk to guard against with a narrower origin allowlist.
10	
11	export const corsHeaders: Record<string, string> = {
12	  "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
13	  "Access-Control-Allow-Headers":
14	    "authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type",
15	  "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS",
16	};

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user invoked the Ralph autonomous loop iteration for the Marketify project (mobile marketplace MVP: Expo + React Native + Supabase). Each iteration must: (a) orient via `scripts/ralph/prd.json` + `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, (b) pick the lowest-priority `passes: false` story, (c) research/implement the minimal change, (d) run quality gates (`tsc`, `lint`, Supabase advisors), (e) spawn `feature-dev:code-reviewer`, (f) verify via mobile-mcp for UI stories, (g) commit with `feat: [US-XXX] Title`, (h) mark `passes: true`, append progress entry. Hard rule: one story per iteration; "Never skip mobile verification on UI stories"; "If the simulator is unavailable...move to the next story."

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Expo SDK 54, React Native new-arch, expo-router v6 (custom TabBar because `<Tabs>` crashes under Expo Go)
   - Supabase with custom HS256 JWT (secret `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, separate from Supabase's project JWT secret)
   - Deno edge functions with `_shared/jwt.ts` (verifyJwt/signJwt), `_shared/cors.ts`, `_shared/apify.ts`
   - PostgREST RLS with `current_user_id()` / `current_user_role()` helpers reading `auth.jwt()`
   - Apify integration via REST (`clockworks~tiktok-scraper`, `apify~instagram-scraper`)
   - Supabase Realtime with explicit `realtime.setAuth(token)` for custom-JWT wrapper
   - Reanimated worklets + `useReducedMotion` + `withReducedMotion(config, reduced)` helper
   - Eligibility engine per docs/tech-architecture.md §6: metrics (`min_followers`, `min_avg_views_last_n`, `min_total_likes`, `min_videos_posted`, `verified_only`), fail-closed on null
   - Seed fixtures: creator `11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002`, listing `11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111010`, version `11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111011`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`scripts/ralph/progress.txt`** (read, not modified)
      - Contains 108 lines of "Codebase Patterns" — the canonical learnings consolidated across iterations. Key patterns: no emoji, one role per account, migrations append-only, RLS mandatory, JWT secret separate from Supabase project secret, Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`, etc.

   - **`scripts/ralph/prd.json`** (read, NOT yet modified)
      - US-036 `passes: false` with existing notes about JWT secret drift blocker
      - US-038 `passes: false` (Discover feed — blocked by same issue, discover.tsx doesn't exist yet)
      - US-039 `passes: false` — target for this iteration

   - **`app/(auth)/_layout.tsx`** (read only, pre-existing bug discovered)
      - Redirects creators to `/(creator)/discover` which doesn't exist. This is why dev-bypass signin landed on "Unmatched Route". Pre-existing from US-031; will be resolved when US-038 lands.

   - **`app/(creator)/profile.tsx`** (read only — US-036 code already committed)
      - Lines 62-110: load flow queries `creator_profiles`, `social_links`, `metric_snapshots` via PostgREST — all gated by the drifted JWT secret.

   - **`supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts`** (read as reference pattern)
      - Used as template for US-039: JWT verification via `verifyJwt`, CORS via `corsHeaders`, `jsonResponse(status, body)` helper, `bearerToken(req)` extractor, service-role supabase client, error shape `{error: "CODE"}`.

   - **`scripts/test/metrics-refresh.ts`** (read as smoke-test template)
      - Used `signMarketifyJwt`, `b64url`, `post`, `expect` helpers.

   - **`supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`** (CREATED — ~280 lines)
      - POST handler: verify creator JWT → validate listing_id UUID → service-role query listings/conditions/videos/creator_profiles/applications in parallel → evaluate PRE conditions → return split pre/post conditions + sample_videos + eligibility
      - Core eligibility evaluator:
      ```ts
      function evaluatePreCondition(condition, metrics): FailedCondition | null {
        if (operator === "gte") {
          const required = Number(condition.numeric_threshold);
          const actual = readNumericMetric(metrics, platform, metric);
          if (actual === null || actual < required) {
            return { metric, platform, required, actual };
          }
        }
        if (operator === "bool") {
          const required = condition.bool_threshold === true;
          const actual = readBoolMetric(metrics, platform, metric);
          if (actual !== required) return { metric, platform, required, actual };
        }
        return null;
      }
      ```
      - Response: `{listing, pre_conditions, post_conditions, sample_videos, eligibility: {eligible, failed_conditions, has_active_application}}`
      - Draft listings return 404 to non-owners
      - Fail-closed on null metrics per §3d

   - **`scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts`** (CREATED — ~250 lines)
      - 10 assertions: missing auth 401, non-bearer 401, wrong-secret 401, lister-role 403, malformed JSON 400, missing listing_id 400, non-UUID 400, unknown listing 404, happy path 200 eligible:true, ineligible case (set tiktok_follower_count=500, verify 200 eligible:false with tiktok min_followers failure)
      - Uses seed fixtures, snapshots/restores creator profile

   - **`scripts/test/_probe_postgrest.ts`** (CREATED then DELETED — one-off verification)
      - Signed up a creator via `auth-signup-creator`, used returned JWT against `/rest/v1/creator_profiles` — got PGRST301 confirming blocker.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **"Missing env" in probe script**: First placed at `/tmp/postgrest_probe.ts` — bun auto-loads `.env` only from CWD. Fixed by moving to `scripts/test/_probe_postgrest.ts`, but then discovered `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` is NOT in local `.env` (only set as Supabase edge-function secret). Solved by instead calling `auth-signup-creator` to get a real JWT.
   - **"Unmatched Route" after dev-bypass signin**: Discovered `app/(auth)/_layout.tsx` redirects creators to `/(creator)/discover` which doesn't exist. Pre-existing bug from US-031; will resolve with US-038.
   - **Supabase edge-function deploy failed** (immediately before summary request): 
     ```
     Module not found "file:///tmp/user_fn_.../_shared/jwt.ts" at source/index.ts:47:27
     ```
     Caused by deploying only `index.ts` without the shared helpers. Fix: include `_shared/jwt.ts` and `_shared/cors.ts` in the `files` array of the deploy call. Did not retry yet.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Solved:** Confirmed US-036 blocker is operator-level (Supabase dashboard JWT secret realignment required) via direct PostgREST probe returning PGRST301.
   - **Solved:** Pivoted to US-039 which has no mobile verification requirement.
   - **In progress:** Deploy edge function with correct file manifest.
   - **Ongoing:** US-036 and US-038 remain blocked until operator realigns Supabase project JWT secret with `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`.

6. All user messages (non-tool results):
   - Initial message: Standard Ralph loop SessionStart context with superpowers skill instructions, deferred tool list, MCP server instructions, available skills list, and the Marketify CLAUDE.md contents (both global and project-level).
   - System reminder about TodoWrite use (×5, ignored per instructions).
   - System reminder that `LSP` tool is no longer available.
   - System reminder at the end (the current turn): "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools." — requesting this summary.

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Retry `deploy_edge_function` with `index.ts` + `_shared/jwt.ts` + `_shared/cors.ts` all in the files array
   - Run smoke test `bun run scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts` — need to verify all 10 assertions pass
   - Run quality gates: `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npx expo lint`
   - Spawn `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with US-039 diff scope
   - Update US-036 notes in prd.json to document today's re-confirmation of the JWT secret blocker
   - Set US-039 `passes: true` in prd.json
   - Append progress entry for US-039 in progress.txt
   - Commit with message `feat: [US-039] Edge function: get-listing-detail`

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before the summary request, I attempted to deploy the newly-created `get-listing-detail` edge function via the Supabase MCP tool `deploy_edge_function`. The deploy FAILED with:
   ```
   Failed to bundle the function (reason: Module not found 
   "file:///tmp/user_fn_[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]_.../_shared/jwt.ts".
   at file:///tmp/user_fn_.../source/index.ts:47:27)
   ```
   The edge function imports `../_shared/jwt.ts` and `../_shared/cors.ts` but my deploy call only included `index.ts` in the `files` array. The function is written locally at `supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts` but has not yet been deployed to the Supabase project `[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Retry the edge function deploy with the shared dependencies included. The deploy call needs `files: [{name: "index.ts", content: ...}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts", content: ...}, {name: "_shared/cors.ts", content: ...}]`. After successful deploy, run `bun run scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts` to verify all 10 smoke-test assertions pass. Then continue with quality gates, code review, and commit per the Ralph loop discipline. This continues the task I was on immediately before the summary was requested: completing US-039 (the `get-listing-detail` edge function) since US-036 was blocked by the documented JWT-secret-drift infrastructure issue.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/bulutyazgan/.claude/projects/-Users-bulutyazgan-Desktop-marketify/74fdc3cd-157b-4c51-961c-42a942a94a27.jsonl

# Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Each invocation is a fresh Claude Code instance with no prior memory. Every persistent decision lives in git history, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, and `scripts/ralph/prd.json`.

---

## Authoritative specs (read these for any ambiguity)

| File | What it defines |
|---|---|
| `docs/product-plan.md` | Canonical product, 12-step build sequence, scope cuts |
| `docs/design.md` | Visual language, component inventory, screen catalog, motion |
| `docs/tech-architecture.md` | DB schema (§4.7), RLS, triggers, Apify integration (§3), edge-function spec, eligibility engine |

If a story is ambiguous, the specs win. Do **not** invent product behavior the docs don't sanction. If something is genuinely missing, append a `Spec gap:` note to `progress.txt` and pick the simplest interpretation that aligns with the docs.

---

## The loop you run, every iteration

1. **Orient.** Read `scripts/ralph/prd.json` and `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`. Pay special attention to the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt` — it consolidates everything previous iterations learned.
2. **Branch.** Make sure you're on the branch named in `prd.json.branchName`. If it doesn't exist, create it from `main`.
3. **Pick one story.** Lowest `priority` number where `passes: false`. Work on **exactly one** story per iteration.
4. **Research before coding.** If understanding the story requires reading >3 files, **delegate to the Explore subagent** (`subagent_type: "Explore"`) so your own context stays clean for implementation. Pass it the story plus pointers into the docs.
5. **Implement.** Make the minimal change set that satisfies every acceptance criterion. Match existing conventions. Don't refactor adjacent code.
6. **Quality gates** (run in this order, stop on first failure and fix before continuing):
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` — must pass with zero errors
   - `npx expo lint` — must pass (warnings allowed only if pre-existing in main)
   - Migration sanity: any new SQL must apply cleanly via Supabase MCP without manual fix-up
7. **Code review (mandatory before commit).** Spawn the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with the diff scope, the story acceptance criteria, and the relevant doc sections. Address every issue rated medium or higher. Re-run quality gates after fixes.
8. **Mobile verification (mandatory for any UI story).** Use `mobile-mcp` to:
   - Launch the app on the iOS simulator (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_launch_app`)
   - Navigate to the screen the story touches
   - Exercise the golden path
   - Take a screenshot (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_take_screenshot`) and reference it in the progress note
   - If the simulator is unavailable, **do not silently skip** — set `passes: false`, write a `notes` entry on the story explaining the blocker, and move to the next story.
9. **Commit.** Stage everything you changed (no `git add -A` blanket — list paths). Commit message format:
   ```
   feat: [US-XXX] Story title
   ```
10. **Mark done.** In `prd.json`, set `passes: true` for the story you just finished. Append a dated entry to `progress.txt` (format below).
11. **Stop check.** If every story has `passes: true`, your final response must contain `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` and nothing else useful. Otherwise end normally — the next iteration picks up.

---

## MCP servers — use these instead of CLI wherever possible

### Supabase (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`) — required for ALL database work
- `apply_migration` — every schema change. Migrations are append-only and named after the story (e.g. `us_006_metric_snapshots_denorm_trigger`).
- `execute_sql` — read-only queries, RLS verification, seed data inspection.
- `list_tables`, `list_migrations`, `list_extensions` — orient before changing things.
- `generate_typescript_types` — regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts` after every migration. Commit the regenerated file with the migration.
- `deploy_edge_function`, `get_edge_function`, `list_edge_functions` — for `apify-core`, `auth-*`, `metrics-refresh`, the webhook receiver, etc.
- `get_advisors` — run with `type: "security"` after any schema or RLS change. Address every error-level advisor before committing.
- `get_logs` — when an edge function or query misbehaves.

Do **not** use the local `supabase` CLI for migrations or queries. The MCP is the source of truth so iterations stay consistent.

### Mobile (`mcp__mobile-mcp__*`) — required for any UI verification
- `mobile_list_available_devices` then `mobile_launch_app` to boot the app.
- `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` + `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` to drive flows.
- `mobile_take_screenshot` after each meaningful state — name screenshots `us-XXX-state.png` and store under `scripts/ralph/screenshots/`.
- Use `mobile_type_keys` rather than tapping virtual keys when filling text inputs.

### Context7 (`mcp__plugin_context7_context7__*`) — for any library docs
Use **before** writing non-trivial integration code with Expo, Reanimated, NativeWind, Supabase JS, React Navigation, etc. Your training data may be stale. `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs`.

### Apify
The API key is in `.env` as `APIFY_KEY`. Always read it at runtime via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` (or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` inside edge functions). **Never** paste the key into source. Actor catalog and input schemas live in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §3.

---

## Subagent delegation — preserve your context

You will burn out your own context window if you try to do everything in-line. Delegate aggressively:

| When | Subagent | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Need to read >3 files to understand the codebase | `Explore` | Returns a focused summary instead of dumping raw file contents into your context |
| Before every commit | `feature-dev:code-reviewer` | Independent review against acceptance criteria + doc sections |
| Implementation will touch >5 files (e.g. wiring a new edge function end-to-end) | `feature-dev:code-architect` first, then implement yourself | Plan once, execute once |
| Need to research how something works before changing it | `feature-dev:code-explorer` | Maps the existing layers without polluting your context |
| Open-ended search across the repo | `general-purpose` | Iterates until it finds the answer |

When briefing a subagent, give it the story ID, acceptance criteria, and a pointer to the relevant `docs/*.md` section. Do **not** tell it "based on your findings, implement" — synthesize the result yourself.

---

## Project quality conventions (these become permanent — additions go in `progress.txt` Codebase Patterns)

- **TypeScript everywhere.** No `any` without a `// eslint-disable-next-line` and a comment explaining why. Generated Supabase types are imported from `src/types/supabase.ts`.
- **Migrations are append-only.** Never edit a previously applied migration. If you need to change something, write a new migration that alters the prior state.
- **Edge functions** live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`. Each one has a one-paragraph header comment naming the contract (request shape → response shape) and the auth requirement.
- **RLS is mandatory** on every table that holds user data. After applying any RLS migration, run `get_advisors({type: "security"})` and fix every finding before committing.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific composites in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji** anywhere in the product UI (per `docs/design.md` — Lucide icons only).
- **No payments code** in v1 — `price_cents` is display-only.
- **Dual-role accounts** are out of scope for v1. One role per account.

---

## Progress log format (`progress.txt`)

Append (never replace) using this format:

```
## YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — US-XXX
- What was implemented (1–3 bullets, concrete)
- Files changed (bulleted list)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: [approved | issues fixed in this commit]
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered
  - Gotchas
  - Useful pointers
---
```

If a learning is **general and reusable across stories**, also promote it into the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt`. That section is what every future iteration leans on first — keep it tight, deduplicated, and signal-only.

---

## Stop condition

After updating `prd.json`, count remaining `passes: false` stories. If zero, your last line in this response must be exactly:

```
<promise>COMPLETE</promise>
```

Otherwise end normally and let the loop spawn the next iteration.

---

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **One story per iteration.** Don't peek ahead.
- **Never commit broken code.** A failing typecheck means you don't commit, you don't mark `passes: true`, you write a `notes` entry, and the next iteration retries.
- **Never skip the code-reviewer subagent.** It is the second pair of eyes that keeps quality from drifting across iterations.
- **Never skip mobile verification on UI stories.** A screen "looking right in the JSX" is not the same as it actually rendering correctly.
- **Never read `.env` contents into the conversation.** Reference variable names only.
- **Never edit `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, or `docs/tech-architecture.md`** unless a story explicitly says to. They are specs, not work product.
- **Never invent stories** or add new ones to `prd.json` mid-loop. If you discover a missing piece, append it as a `Spec gap:` line in `progress.txt` so the human can decide.

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

3	// Contract:
4	//   POST { listing_id: string }
5	//     → 200 {
6	//         listing:          <row from public.listings>,
7	//         pre_conditions:   <rows from listing_conditions where kind='pre'>,
8	//         post_conditions:  <rows from listing_conditions where kind='post'>,
9	//         sample_videos:    <rows from sample_videos>,
10	//         eligibility: {
11	//           eligible:                boolean,
12	//           failed_conditions:       Array<{metric, platform, required, actual}>,

14	//         }

17	//     → 401 { error: 'UNAUTHORIZED' }
18	//     → 403 { error: 'FORBIDDEN' }       // non-creator role
19	//     → 404 { error: 'LISTING_NOT_FOUND' } // also covers draft listings for non-owners
20	//     → 500 { error: 'SERVER_MISCONFIGURED' | 'DB_ERROR' }
21	//
22	// Auth: Marketify JWT (HS256, MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET). Verifies via
23	//       _shared/jwt.ts and asserts role='creator'. Deployed with
24	//       verify_jwt=false at the gateway because the gateway only knows
25	//       about Supabase's own JWT secret — the handler enforces auth.
26	//

28	// (the spec's `failed_conditions: Array<{metric, required, actual}>` wins

44	// as ineligible (fail-closed).") and we apply the same rule uniformly.

input.prompt:
Review US-039 — a new Supabase edge function `get-listing-detail` plus its smoke test. The function returns a listing, its split pre/post conditions, sample videos, and an eligibility decision for the authenticated creator.

## Files in the review scope

1. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts` (NEW, ~339 lines)
2. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts` (NEW, ~327 lines)

Nothing else has been modified — this is an isolated edge-function addition.

## Authoritative specs

- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/tech-architecture.md` §5.4 (listing-detail response shape, `failed_conditions: Array<{metric, required, actual}>` is the authoritative shape — it supersedes the user story's `reasons: string[]`)
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/tech-architecture.md` §6.3 (pre-conditions gate eligibility at post/apply time; post-conditions evaluated at submission; only pre-conditions should drive `eligible`/`failed_conditions`)
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/tech-architecture.md` §3d (fail-closed on null metrics)
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/product-plan.md` for scope/no-payments rules

## Story acceptance criteria (US-039)

See `scripts/ralph/prd.json` entry for US-039. The key ACs:
- Creator JWT required; non-creator → 403; bad JWT → 401
- Unknown or draft listing → 404
- Response includes the listing row, separate `pre_conditions` and `post_conditions` arrays from the single `listing_conditions` table (discriminated by `kind`), `sample_videos`, and an `eligibility` block with `eligible`, `failed_conditions[]`, and `has_active_application`
- Fail-closed on null metrics
- `has_active_application` reflects any pending/approved application by the caller for this listing

## Sibling functions for convention reference

The function follows the same patterns as:
- `supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts` (JWT verify, cors, service-role client, jsonResponse helper, bearerToken helper — identical shape used here)
- `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts`
- `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256 verifier)
- `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`

## What I want you to check

1. **Correctness of eligibility evaluation** — does `evaluatePreCondition` match what the spec requires? Are the metric keys in `readNumericMetric` / `readBoolMetric` the correct column names? Is fail-closed behavior right (null actual → fail; malformed required → skip, not fail — is "skip" the right call here or should it fail?)?
2. **Information leakage** — does the 404 vs 403 choice across the error surface leak listing existence across listers? (Drafts collapse to 404 intentionally.)
3. **Concurrency / races** — the `has_active_application` check is `status in ('pending','approved')`. Is that the correct set of "active" statuses? Check against the `applications.status` check constraint in the migrations (`supabase/migrations/`).
4. **Null safety** — `profileRes.data ?? {...}` fallback. Does the Postgres schema guarantee a creator_profiles row exists for every creator user_id? If yes, the fallback is overkill; if no, this is the right call.
5. **`current_version_id` guard** — the function treats a missing `current_version_id` on a non-draft listing as DB_ERROR. Is that accurate per the schema/trigger? Look at the listings table schema and any publish trigger.
6. **Response shape fidelity** — against §5.4 of tech-architecture.md, does the response match exactly? Are `pre_conditions` and `post_conditions` the right split point, or does the spec expect them to be named differently?
7. **Parallel query correctness** — the Promise.all fans out conditions/videos/profile/apps. Any missed error path or race?
8. **Smoke-test coverage** — does the test actually verify the happy path's response shape thoroughly? Any obvious gaps?
9. **Bundler/deploy concern** — the function imports from `../_shared/jwt.ts` and `../_shared/cors.ts`. Is this exactly how the sibling functions import? (It should be — copy-paste from metrics-refresh.)

Please rate findings by confidence and priority. Report only medium/high-priority issues. Low-confidence nits can be bundled under a single "nits" heading.

Do NOT implement fixes — just report findings. I'll synthesize and apply them.

content.text:
I now have all the data needed. Here is the full review.

---

## Review: US-039 `get-listing-detail` edge function

Files reviewed:
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts`

---

## Critical Issues

### 1. Response shape diverges from §5.4 spec — `conditions` vs. `pre_conditions` / `post_conditions` (Confidence: 95)

`docs/tech-architecture.md` §5.4 defines the `GET /listings/:id` response as:

```ts
interface Res {
  listing: Listing;
  conditions: ListingCondition[];   // <-- single array, not split
  sample_videos: SampleVideo[];
  eligibility: { ... };
}
```

The implementation returns `pre_conditions` and `post_conditions` as two separate top-level keys. The story AC says "separate `pre_conditions` and `post_conditions` arrays" and the function's own header comment says the split happens at the edge per the story AC — but the authoritative shape in §5.4 has a single `conditions: ListingCondition[]`. The instruction given in this review says "§5.4 is the authoritative shape… it supersedes the user story's `reasons: string[]`", so the spec wins over the story AC on shape questions. This is a direct spec violation on the envelope key name.

The practical impact is that the client built against §5.4 would break. The fix is either to return a single `conditions` array (with `kind` preserved on each row for the client to split), or to treat the story AC as an intentional override and document it as a spec gap. Given the review brief explicitly calls §5.4 authoritative, the current output shape is non-conformant.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`, lines 328–338.

---

### 2. `failed_conditions` shape in §5.4 does not include `platform` — but the implementation adds it (Confidence: 85)

§5.4 specifies:
```ts
failed_conditions: Array<{ metric; required; actual }>
```

The `FailedCondition` interface (line 80–85) and the returned objects also carry `platform`. The §6.2 evaluator signature in the spec includes `platform` on the `Condition` input, but the `failed_conditions` output in §5.4 only lists `metric`, `required`, `actual`. Since the review brief says §5.4 wins, the extra `platform` field is a spec deviation. Practically this is additive and unlikely to break a client, but it is a departure from the specified shape.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`, lines 80–85 and 118–119.

---

### 3. Paused / closed / archived listings are served as valid detail responses — visibility model is underspecified but potentially wrong (Confidence: 82)

The function 404s only on `status === 'draft'` (line 242). All other statuses — `paused`, `closed`, `archived` — return a full 200. The spec's listing-detail contract in §5.4 does not explicitly address visibility of non-draft, non-active listings, but the product plan describes `paused` and `closed` as operator states (lister-only actions), and `archived` as a terminal state. A creator hitting the detail endpoint for an archived listing will receive `eligible: true` but the Apply CTA driven by this endpoint would be nonsensical.

More importantly, `archived` is terminal per §2.1 and the application state machine has `cancelled_listing_closed` but no path from `archived → can apply`. If the function returns `eligible: true` for an archived listing, the downstream `apply-to-listing` edge function will need to guard against it, or the creator will receive a 200 with an effectively dead listing. The `status` field is present on the returned `listing` row so the client can check, but the function itself provides no signal (not a 404, not a 410) to distinguish "listing exists and is worth showing" from "listing is closed/archived."

This is a potential product-correctness issue depending on what US-040 (the client screen) does with non-active listings. At minimum, `archived` listings should collapse to 404 by the same logic as drafts.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`, lines 239–244.

---

## Important Issues

### 4. `current_version_id` guard does not account for `paused`/`closed` listings set before the bump trigger existed (Confidence: 82)

The trigger `bump_listing_version` only sets `current_version_id` when a versioned field changes AND `new.status = 'active'`. A listing that was published (transitioning from `draft → active`) sets `current_version_id` via the create-listing edge function (not the trigger). If a listing was `paused` or `closed` before the cascade trigger landed (us_010 migration), its `current_version_id` could be NULL — the guard at line 247 would then return `DB_ERROR 500` for a valid listing. This is correct fail-safe behavior, but the comment on line 249 says "every non-draft listing should have a current version by trigger (us_010)" which is inaccurate — the publish step, not the bump trigger, is what guarantees `current_version_id` is set on the first version. The code behavior is fine; the comment is misleading and will confuse future maintainers.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`, lines 246–253.

---

### 5. Unknown-operator pre-conditions silently pass instead of failing closed (Confidence: 88)

`evaluatePreCondition` (line 134–136) returns `null` (pass) for any operator that isn't `gte` or `bool`. The comment says "skip rather than fail" and attributes this to "Other operators are reserved for post-conditions." However, this function is called only for `kind='pre'` conditions. If a future migration adds a `lte` or `eq` pre-condition (or a data-entry error inserts one), the function will silently pass it — producing `eligible: true` when the creator may actually fail.

The spec §3d establishes fail-closed as the baseline for missing data. There is no spec statement that says "fail-open for unknown operators on pre-conditions." The safer default here would be to return a fail entry for unknown operators (treating the condition as failing closed), not skip. The distinction matters most if a pre-condition row gets a misconfigured operator through a future migration.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`, lines 131–136.

---

### 6. `has_active_application` query uses `.limit(1)` but checks `appsRes.data.length` without error-checking the case where `data` is null while `error` is also null (Confidence: 80)

The check on line 326 is `(appsRes.data ?? []).length > 0`. This is safe because of the `?? []` fallback. However, the pattern is subtly inconsistent: the `conditionsRes`, `videosRes`, `profileRes` checks each explicitly guard `if (X.error)` before using `.data`. The `appsRes` path does the same (line 299–300). This is fine — the `?? []` handles the null-data case. No actual bug, but the combined `.limit(1)` + length check could be replaced with `(appsRes.data?.length ?? 0) > 0` for consistency. This is a nit, not a bug.

---

### 7. `numeric_threshold` is typed as `string | number | null` in `ConditionRow` but Postgres returns it as a JS number via supabase-js for `numeric` columns (Confidence: 80)

The `listing_conditions.numeric_threshold` column is type `numeric` in the schema (migration line 80). Supabase-js converts Postgres `numeric` to a JavaScript string (not a number) to avoid IEEE-754 precision loss. The code then does `Number(condition.numeric_threshold)` at line 115, which will correctly parse either a string `"1000"` or a number `1000`. The string union in the type is actually correct for supabase-js's behavior. However the comment/doc on this is absent, and any future consumer of `ConditionRow` may be surprised. Not a bug, but worth noting.

---

## Smoke Test Gaps

### 8. Test does not verify `failed_conditions` shape fields in case 10 (Confidence: 85)

Case 10 (ineligibility path, line 301–313) checks `ttFail.required === 1000` and `ttFail.actual === 500`, but does not verify that `ttFail.metric === "min_followers"` or `ttFail.platform === "tiktok"` as independent assertions. It does use `.find(f => f.metric === "min_followers" && f.platform === "tiktok")` to locate the entry, so if the find returns undefined the `!!ttFail` check catches it. But if `required` or `actual` is a string `"1000"` / `"500"` (because `numeric_threshold` comes back as a string from Postgres and is coerced via `Number()` to a number, while `actual` comes from `tiktok_follower_count` which is an `integer` column that supabase-js returns as a JS number), the `=== 1000` / `=== 500` strict equality would still pass. The concern is the reverse: if the function returned `required` as the raw string `"1000"`, `ttFail.required === 1000` would be `false`. This is an implicit test of the `Number()` conversion in `evaluatePreCondition`.

More importantly, case 9 (happy path) does not verify the shape of any individual `pre_conditions` or `post_conditions` row (e.g., that `kind` field values are correct, that `platform` is present, that `metric` is a string). It checks array lengths and `every(c => c.kind === "pre")` which is good, but does not spot-check a known condition row's `operator` or `numeric_threshold` value.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts`, lines 277–293 and 301–313.

---

## Bundler / Convention Confirmation

The import paths `../_ shared/jwt.ts` and `../_shared/cors.ts` at lines 47–48 match the pattern used by `metrics-refresh/index.ts` (lines 85–86) exactly. The `jsonResponse` and `bearerToken` helpers are identical to those in the sibling function. No concerns here.

---

## Information Leakage Confirmation

The draft-collapses-to-404 behavior at line 242 is correct per the spec's intent. A creator querying `listing_id` for a draft (or non-existent) listing gets the same `404 LISTING_NOT_FOUND` regardless, so listing existence for drafts does not leak across lister boundaries. The `maybeSingle()` approach also means a 500 is returned on a multi-row result (shouldn't happen given UUID PK) rather than silently using the first row.

---

## Concurrency / `has_active_application` Status Set

The `.in("status", ["pending", "approved"])` query matches the `applications_open_uniq` partial index in the migration at `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417110000_us_006_pipeline_tables.sql` line 28–30, which is defined `where status in ('pending','approved')`. The `application_status` enum includes `withdrawn`, `rejected`, `cancelled_listing_edit`, `cancelled_listing_closed` — none of those should count as "active." The chosen set is correct and aligns with the unique index definition.

---

## `creator_profiles` Null-Fallback Correctness

The signup RPC `auth_signup_creator` (migration `20260417220000_us_019`) always inserts a `creator_profiles` row atomically with `users` (line 41–42). The transaction rolls back entirely on failure, so no creator JWT can exist without a matching `creator_profiles` row. The `profileRes.data ?? {...null metrics}` fallback at lines 309–318 is defensive dead code for the normal path. However, it is the correct behavior if this function is ever called before the signup RPC fully commits, or in test scenarios with manually crafted JWTs. The fail-closed behavior of the null-metric fallback is correct per §3d and represents no bug.

---

## Summary of Actionable Findings

| # | Severity | Confidence | Short Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Critical | 95 | Response envelope uses `pre_conditions`/`post_conditions` but §5.4 specifies `conditions` (single array) |
| 2 | Important | 85 | `failed_conditions` items include `platform` which is absent from §5.4's specified shape |
| 3 | Important | 82 | Non-active statuses (`paused`, `closed`, `archived`) return 200; `archived` at minimum should 404 |
| 5 | Important | 88 | Unknown pre-condition operators silently pass (fail-open) rather than failing closed per §3d |
| 4 | Low | 82 | Misleading comment on `current_version_id` guard (code is correct; comment attributes guarantee to wrong mechanism) |
| 8 | Low | 85 | Smoke test case 10 does not independently verify `failed_conditions` shape; case 9 does not spot-check a condition row's fields |
